finding_results.notes: Reworked the server-room confrontation so it does new dramatic work rather than replaying the lab scene's accusation-deflection ladder. Haruki now arrives with the facility network diagram (from the data-governance printouts established at line 99), showing a shared VLAN segment with Marcus's signature on the approval line. Marcus's refusal shifts from restating 'the sandbox was standard' to a territorial block: he won't let Haruki into the server room to verify the switch config. The exchange climbs a different ladder — documentary evidence, verification demand, territorial refusal, social threat ('what does that look like upstairs'), and physical ultimatum ('get away from the door') — before arriving at the same shove. The lab scene (lines 103-117) is preserved unchanged as the initial public accusation.

summary: Single finding addressed. The lab argument (lines 103-117) stays as the initial public accusation where Haruki identifies Marcus and makes the technical case. The server-room confrontation (lines 129-149) was reworked so Haruki brings a new piece of evidence — the VLAN diagram with Marcus's signature — and Marcus's refusal is territorial rather than a restatement of the same technical defense. The two scenes now climb different ladders before converging on the same physical outcome.
